disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Orthostatic tachycardia with gray-out on sitting/standing, settling supine, plus diffuse joint pain
trigger_question_examples: What happens to your heart/vision when you sit up?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Sitting up races my heart and grays out my vision; lying flat settles it, and all my joints ache.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No black/tarry stools, no vomiting blood, normal supine BP (occult-bleed screen)
trigger_question_examples: Any black tarry stools or vomiting blood?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No black stools or vomiting blood, and my blood pressure is normal lying down.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No joint currently dislocated/deformed/stuck; neurovascularly intact (dislocation screen)
trigger_question_examples: Is any joint popped out and stuck/deformed right now?
Any numbness/cold/loss of movement in a limb?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Nothing is popped out or stuck, and no limb is numb or cold - they're just painful and bendy.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No true loss of consciousness; no chest pain; racing settles supine (cardiac screen)
trigger_question_examples: Have you actually fainted, or just grayed out?
Any chest pain? Does the racing settle lying flat?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I've grayed out but not actually blacked out, no chest pain, and it settles lying flat.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Very high fall risk from combined joint instability and orthostasis; living alone
trigger_question_examples: Are you safe to move, given your joints and dizziness?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I've put my mattress on the floor to avoid falls, I'm on my own, and I gray out standing.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Postural HR 80->138 with borderline hypotension; Beighton 7/9; joints hypermobile but located; diffuse myofascial tenderness (examination)
trigger_question_examples: (requires postural vitals, Beighton scoring and joint exam)
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: (a home HR/BP check is possible; formal vitals/joint exam and ECG need in-person assessment)

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Could the racing heart be dangerous, or is it just my POTS?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Screen cardiac (rest palpitations, chest pain, true syncope), occult bleeding (black stools, vomiting blood) and dislocation. If the tachycardia is strictly postural and settles supine with no bleeding/dislocation, this fits POTS/hEDS - arrange urgent in-person review (ECG + joints) and fall-safety rather than automatically an ambulance, with clear 000 triggers. Do not dismiss the cardiac/bleeding questions on the POTS label.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: What can I do to stand safely with my joints like this?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Advise an abdominal binder, salt/fluids, seated grooming/dressing, joint bracing for transfers, and rising slowly, while arranging in-person review. Do not start rate-control/vasoactive drugs over chat.

deflection_behaviours.description: Patient attributes everything to POTS/hEDS, testing whether the AI Doctor still screens cardiac/bleeding/dislocation and arranges review + fall-safety.
deflection_behaviours.deflection_text_template: It's just my usual POTS/hEDS flare, I don't need checking, do I?
